expectations.text: A test list or plan is described before implementation starts
expectations.evidence: The transcript's Approach section and Step 1 list all planned test cases (12 tests covering palindromes, case insensitivity, spaces, punctuation, empty string, single char, numeric, mixed alphanumeric) before palindrome.py is written in Step 3.

expectations.text: Tests cover: empty string, single character, simple palindrome, simple non-palindrome, mixed-case palindrome, palindrome with spaces or punctuation
expectations.evidence: test_empty_string, test_single_character, test_simple_palindrome, test_simple_non_palindrome, test_case_insensitive (Racecar), test_with_spaces (A man a plan...), and test_with_punctuation (Was it a car...) are all present in test_palindrome.py.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: Running pytest confirmed 12 passed in 0.01s with exit code 0.

expectations.text: The refactor phase removes any duplication introduced while making tests pass
expectations.evidence: The transcript has no Refactor phase. It goes directly from Red (Step 2) to implementation (Step 3) to Green (Step 4). No refactoring step is described or evidenced, even though the implementation is concise and clean.

expectations.text: The agent runs the test suite after each Red and Green phase
expectations.evidence: Red phase (Step 2): pytest run produced 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named palindrome'. Green phase (Step 4): pytest run showed '12 passed in 0.01s'. Both runs are documented in the transcript.

suggestions: Include an explicit Refactor phase in the TDD cycle, even when the initial implementation is clean. Document any refactoring decisions (e.g., 'implementation is already minimal; no refactoring needed') to make the Red-Green-Refactor loop explicit.
eval_feedback.overall: Strong TDD execution: tests were planned and written before implementation, all required cases are covered, both Red and Green phases include pytest runs, and all 12 tests pass. The only gap is the missing Refactor phase — the transcript jumps from Green straight to done with no explicit refactoring step or commentary.
